labels_origin: planted at corpus generation; never derived from retriever output
relevance: a retrieved chunk is a hit when its source file is in the query's relevant set; labels are never keyed to chunk ids

queries.query: what pressure does the thernwick governor hold at cold start
how often is the thernwick rail re-shimmed
what temperature limit applies to the vashtrin buffer
why does the pellagrove damper need a second anchor
what is the bleed order for the pellagrove manifold
what tool releases the vashtrin escapement
what causes the ganthorpe diaphragm to blister
how is the ganthorpe wickstay tensioned

topics.query: what pressure does the thernwick governor hold at cold start
topics.fact: The thernwick governor holds a cold-start pressure of 47 kilopascal. Crews read the thernwick governor before the first pass of a shift, because a governor that has drifted below 40 kilopascal will not seat.

topics.query: how often is the thernwick rail re-shimmed
topics.fact: The thernwick rail is re-shimmed every nine hundred cycles. Shimming the thernwick rail early wastes a wear allowance that is measured over the whole service interval, not over a single week.

topics.query: what temperature limit applies to the vashtrin buffer
topics.fact: The vashtrin buffer must stay under sixty-two degrees at the outlet. Above that the vashtrin buffer starts to shed its facing, and the loss is not recoverable by cooling it back down afterwards.

topics.query: why does the pellagrove damper need a second anchor
topics.fact: The pellagrove damper needs a second anchor because its first anchor carries only vertical load. Without the second anchor a pellagrove damper walks sideways under reversing load and eventually fouls.

topics.query: what is the bleed order for the pellagrove manifold
topics.fact: The pellagrove manifold is bled outboard first, then centre, then inboard. Bleeding a pellagrove manifold in the reverse order traps a pocket at the centre port that no amount of further bleeding clears.

topics.query: what tool releases the vashtrin escapement
topics.fact: The vashtrin escapement is released with a quarter-inch offset key. Prying a vashtrin escapement with a screwdriver deforms the pawl seat, which is the one part of the assembly with no spare.

topics.query: what causes the ganthorpe diaphragm to blister
topics.fact: The ganthorpe diaphragm blisters when it is seated dry. A ganthorpe diaphragm wetted with the specified film will not blister even after several hundred reversals at full stroke.

topics.query: how is the ganthorpe wickstay tensioned
topics.fact: The ganthorpe wickstay is tensioned to eleven newton metres, in two passes. A ganthorpe wickstay taken to full torque in one pass reads correct on the wrench and is still slack across the far side.

no_answer_query: what clearance does the thernwick escutcheon take when it is refaced
